name: ESP32 Camera Component
description: The `esp32_camera` component allows you to use ESP32-based camera boards in ESPHome that directly integrate into Home Assistant through the native API.

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name of the camera.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the camera in the frontend.

config_entries.label: I2C ID
config_entries.description:
**Required — set exactly one of:** `i2c_pins`, `i2c_id`.

The ID of the [I²C bus](https://esphome.io/components/i2c) the camera is connected to.

config_entries.label: Data Pins
config_entries.description: The data lanes of the camera, this must be a list of 8 GPIO pins.

config_entries.label: External Clock
config_entries.description: The configuration of the external clock to drive the camera.

config_entries.label: Pin
config_entries.description: The pin the external clock line is connected to.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Frequency
config_entries.description: The frequency of the external clock, must be between `8MHz` and `20MHz`. Defaults to `20MHz`.

config_entries.label: Href Pin
config_entries.description: The pin the HREF line of the camera is connected to.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Power Down Pin
config_entries.description: The ESP pin to power down the camera. If set, this will power down the camera while it is inactive.

config_entries.label: Reset Pin
config_entries.description: The ESP pin the reset pin of the camera is connected to. If set, this will reset the camera before the ESP boots.

config_entries.label: VSYNC Pin

config_entries.label: Ae Level
config_entries.description: The auto exposure level to apply to the picture (when **aec_mode** is set to `auto`  ), from -2 to 2. Defaults to `0`.

config_entries.label: Aec2
config_entries.description: Whether to enable Auto Exposure Control 2. Seems to change computation method of automatic exposure. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Aec Mode
config_entries.description: The mode of exposure module. Defaults to `auto` (leave camera to automatically adjust exposure).

config_entries.label: Aec Value
config_entries.description: The Exposure value to apply to the picture (when **aec_mode** is set to `manual`  ), from 0 to 1200. Defaults to `300`.

config_entries.label: Agc Gain Ceiling
config_entries.description: The maximum gain allowed, when **agc_mode** is set to `auto`. This parameter seems act as "ISO" setting. Defaults to `2x`.

config_entries.label: Agc Mode
config_entries.description: The mode of gain control module. Defaults to `auto` (leave camera to automatically adjust sensor gain).

config_entries.label: Agc Value
config_entries.description: The gain value to apply to the picture (when **aec_mode** is set to `manual`  ), from 0 to 30. Defaults to `0`.

config_entries.label: Brightness
config_entries.description: The brightness to apply to the picture, from -2 to 2. Defaults to `0`.

config_entries.label: Contrast
config_entries.description: The contrast to apply to the picture, from -2 to 2. Defaults to `0`.

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Frame Buffer Count
config_entries.description: The number of frame buffers to use when reading from the camera sensor. Must be between 1 and 2. Defaults to `1`.

config_entries.label: Frame Buffer Location
config_entries.description: The memory area used for storing the frame buffers. Defaults to `PSRAM`.

config_entries.label: Horizontal Mirror
config_entries.description: Whether to mirror the image horizontally. Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: Idle Framerate
config_entries.description: The framerate to capture images at when no client is requesting a full stream. Defaults to `0.1 fps`.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: Jpeg Quality
config_entries.description: The JPEG quality that the camera should encode images with. From 6 (best) to 63 (worst). Defaults to `10`. Set this to ``0`` to disable JPEG compression/conversion. Manual JPEG compression/conversion requires `psram` component to be enabled and configured.

config_entries.label: Max Framerate
config_entries.description: The maximum framerate the camera will generate images at. Up to 60Hz is possible (with reduced frame sizes), but beware of overheating. Defaults to `10 fps`.

config_entries.label: Pixel Format
config_entries.description: The pixel format to use for the images. Defaults to ``jpeg`` (JPEG compressed image) which may not be supported by all cameras. Other formats require more CPU and memory and may not be supported by all cameras.

config_entries.label: Resolution
config_entries.description: The resolution the camera will capture images at. Higher resolutions require more memory, if there's not enough memory you will see an error during startup.

config_entries.label: Saturation
config_entries.description: The saturation to apply to the picture, from -2 to 2. Defaults to `0`.

config_entries.label: Special Effect
config_entries.description: The effect to apply to the picture. Defaults to `none` (picture without effect).

config_entries.label: Test Pattern
config_entries.description: For tests purposes, it's possible to replace picture get from sensor by a test color pattern. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Vertical Flip
config_entries.description: Whether to flip the image vertically. Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: Wb Mode
config_entries.description: The mode of white balace module. Defaults to `auto`.
